codeReview.summary: The nil pointer dereference fix in admin.go demote() function is correctly implemented and follows CLAUDE.md patterns. The nil check is placed immediately after the GetMember() call and before MergeChatMember() is invoked, preventing a panic when GetMember returns nil. The test cases in admin_test.go verify the nil handling behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fix correctly follows CLAUDE.md error handling pattern: log.Error(err) followed by return err. The nil check includes a descriptive error message with the userId for debugging.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: alita/modules/admin.go lines 232-235: The nil check logs an error with fmt.Errorf containing userId, then returns the error. This matches the codebase pattern of logging before returning.

sharedStateObservations.observation: In gotgbot v2, ChatMember is an interface type. A nil interface check (userMember == nil) is the correct pattern before calling interface methods like MergeChatMember().
sharedStateObservations.evidence: alita/modules/admin_test.go lines 24-35: The test documents that ChatMember is an interface and demonstrates proper nil interface checking behavior.

summary: Critical nil pointer dereference fix in admin.go demote() is correctly implemented. The nil check after GetMember() prevents panic when calling MergeChatMember(). Test cases verify the fix. Code follows CLAUDE.md patterns. Status: PASS.
